project_identity.name: mintenvoy
project_identity.domain: Desktop tooling for composing, sending, and inspecting HTTP API requests
project_identity.stack: TypeScript on Electron + React, three-process model (main / preload / renderer), bundled by electron-vite and packaged by electron-builder

architecture_rules.title: Process Boundaries
architecture_rules.description: mintEnvoy runs as three Electron processes — main (Node.js), preload (bridge), and renderer (React UI) — that communicate only over IPC; the renderer never touches Node APIs directly.

rules.text: Main process owns app lifecycle, windows, native APIs, persistence (electron-store), and auto-update (electron-updater).
Renderer is sandboxed React UI; it reaches privileged capability only through the preload bridge.
Outbound HTTP (undici) runs in main, not the renderer; the renderer requests it over IPC.

rows: Lifecycle, windows, native APIs, electron-store, electron-updater

architecture_rules.title: IPC & Security
architecture_rules.description: All main-renderer communication crosses the preload contextBridge; contextIsolation stays on and nodeIntegration stays off.

rules.text: Expose the IPC surface from src/preload via contextBridge.exposeInMainWorld; never enable nodeIntegration in the renderer.
Validate and narrow every IPC payload at the boundary before use.

code_examples.label: CORRECT
code_examples.annotation: preload exposes a typed API surface

code_examples.label: WRONG
code_examples.annotation: renderer reaching electron directly bypasses isolation

architecture_rules.title: Module Organization & Imports
architecture_rules.description: Renderer modules resolve through the @renderer alias defined in electron.vite.config.ts.

rules.text: @renderer resolves to src/renderer/src (electron.vite.config.ts); import renderer modules via the alias, not deep relative paths.
Keep code in its process dir: src/main, src/preload, src/renderer — no cross-process imports except the preload-exposed API.

code_quality_standards.title: Type Safety
code_quality_standards.description: TypeScript runs in strict mode (@electron-toolkit/tsconfig base) across node + web configs.

rules.text: strict is on; do not weaken it per-file. Type-check via 'npm run typecheck' (node + web) before completing a task.
No 'any'. Type external/IPC input as 'unknown' and narrow with a type guard.

code_examples.label: CORRECT

rules.text: Never swallow errors — no empty catch; handle, re-throw, or log with reason.
Handle both success and error paths of every fallible operation.
IPC handlers in main catch and convert errors into structured results the renderer can render — don't leak raw stack traces across the bridge.

code_quality_standards.title: Naming Conventions
code_quality_standards.description: Consistent naming across React, hooks, stores, and IPC channels.

code_quality_standards.title: Testing Requirements
code_quality_standards.description: No test infrastructure exists yet (testings: N/A).

rules.text: When adding tests, cover acceptance criteria including edge + error paths; follow the chosen runner's existing patterns once established.
Pick a renderer test stack (e.g. Vitest + Testing Library) before the first feature that needs coverage; record it in docs/architecture.md.

code_quality_standards.title: Documentation
code_quality_standards.description: Code discovery routes through codebase-memory-mcp; new code carries clear docs.

rules.text: Structural code queries use codebase-memory-mcp tools (search_graph / trace_path / get_code_snippet / search_code / query_graph), not raw Read/Grep/Glob over source — CBM hooks block raw discovery on first match per session.
docs/ is LLM-context-source first, dev-greppable second; structural metadata stays in CBM, not embedded in docs/.
All new functions and exported types carry clear documentation.
One responsibility per function; extract when a function grows past ~40 lines or mixes concerns.
SOLID, DRY (don't repeat logic 3+ times), KISS.

always_universal.text: Validate inputs at module boundaries; trust internal code.
Read a file before modifying it.
Handle both success and error paths of every fallible operation.

always_project_specific.text: Shared renderer state lives in a zustand store.
Reach privileged capability only through the preload-exposed API.
Run outbound HTTP (undici) in main; the renderer requests it over IPC.

never_universal.text: Never commit secrets, API keys, or tokens.
Never leave debug artifacts (console.log, debugger) behind.

never_project_specific.text: Never enable nodeIntegration in the renderer.
Never import Node or electron directly in renderer code.
Never mutate zustand state outside its store actions.

prefer_project_specific.text: Prefer zustand selectors over reading the whole store.
Prefer the @renderer alias over deep relative imports.
Prefer typed IPC wrappers over raw ipcRenderer calls in components.

domain_rules.title: Key Entities
domain_rules.description: The API-client domain centers on a small set of entities (design intent — not yet implemented in the scaffold).

rules.text: Request — an HTTP call definition (method, URL, headers, body).
Response — the result of sending a Request (status, headers, body, timing).
Collection — a saved, organized group of Requests.
Environment — a named set of variables substituted into Requests at send time.

workflow_rules.title: Minimal Changes
workflow_rules.description: Keep every change as small as possible.

rules.text: Every change impacts as little code as possible; don't fix unrelated code you happen to see.

workflow_rules.title: Read Before Write
workflow_rules.description: Understand a file before changing it.

rules.text: Always read a file before modifying it.
Before writing anything generic/reusable, search the codebase for an existing utility, helper, or component that already does it.

workflow_rules.title: One Task At A Time
workflow_rules.description: Sequential execution along the dependency graph.

rules.text: Execute tasks sequentially along the dependency graph; finish and verify one before starting the next.
Before each task, read constitution.md + .devforge/memory.md so the task starts with the right context.

workflow_rules.title: Spec-Driven Workflow
workflow_rules.description: Enforcement level: Strict — every pipeline step requires explicit approval.

rules.text: Follow /specify, /plan, /breakdown, /implement, /review, /verify, /finalize; each hard gate needs explicit approval before advancing.
Commits follow Conventional Commits; WIP commits squash into one clean feature commit at /finalize.
Commit messages include the AI attribution footer.
